name: Mousseline Au Chocolat
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg yolks and sugar together until the mixture is thick, pale yellow, and forms a slowly dissolving ribbon.
2. Add the orange liqueur to the yolk mixture.
3. Set the mixing bowl over not-quite-simmering water and continue beating for 3-4 minutes until the mixture is foamy and too hot to touch with your finger.
4. Remove from heat and beat over cold water for another 3-4 minutes until the mixture is cool and forms the ribbon again, having a consistency similar to mayonnaise.
5. Melt the chocolate with coffee over hot water. Remove from heat and add the butter gradually, stirring until you have a smooth cream.
6. Beat in the melted chocolate into the egg yolk mixture, then fold in the optional orange peel.
7. Whisk the egg whites and salt until soft peaks form. Gradually add the sugar and whisk until stiff peaks are formed.
8. Stir 1/4 of the beaten egg whites into the chocolate mixture.
9. Gently fold in the remaining egg whites.
10. Transfer the batter to a serving dish, dessert cups, or petits pots.
11. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
12. Serve with sauce or whipped cream on the side.
